name: Netlify
one_liner: Netlify is a cloud platform for building, deploying, and hosting web apps and serverless functions, with a REST API for managing sites, deploys, env vars, forms, and hooks.
access_notes: A free Netlify account can generate Personal Access Tokens in the UI (Applications > Personal access tokens), no payment or approval required. OAuth2 apps can also be registered self-serve for third-party integrations. Free tier is production-usable.
api_notes: REST API at https://api.netlify.com/api/v1/ documented via OpenAPI (open-api.netlify.com). Covers sites (CRUD), deploys (file digest/ZIP, status, rollback), environment variables (site + team), forms & submissions, snippets, DNS, and hooks. Official Go and JS client libraries. Rate limits: 500 req/min general, 3 deploys/min and 100 deploys/day.
mcp_notes: Netlify ships an official MCP server (github.com/netlify/netlify-mcp), launched June 2025. Remote or local; e.g. `claude mcp add --transport http netlify https://netlify-mcp.netlify.app/mcp`. Lets agents deploy, manage env vars/domains/functions, and install extensions.
main_blocker: None significant. Public REST API with self-serve free credentials (PAT). Deploy rate limits (100/day) could constrain high-volume deploy automation.

evidence.claim: Netlify offers a REST API at https://api.netlify.com/api/v1/ with Personal Access Tokens and OAuth2 auth; rate limit 500 req/min, deploys 3/min and 100/day.
Netlify ships an official MCP server in the netlify/netlify-mcp repository.
Netlify launched its official MCP server (June 2025) enabling AI agents to deploy and manage apps.

open_questions: Exact total operation count (estimated MODERATE from documented resource groups); whether any endpoints are restricted to paid/enterprise plans (e.g. team-level features).
